title: Rate limiter keys on X-Real-IP: trivially bypassed without reverse proxy
description:
server.rs:441-448: Rate limiting keys on X-Real-IP header, falling back to X-Forwarded-For, then 'unknown'.

When broadside is run without a reverse proxy (common in dev/small deployments):
- X-Real-IP is not set by any trusted intermediary
- The attacker sets their own X-Real-IP header to an arbitrary value
- Each request with a different spoofed X-Real-IP value gets a fresh full bucket (60 req/min)
- Effective rate limit: unlimited

Even with a reverse proxy, X-Forwarded-For is also accepted as fallback (line 443), and X-Forwarded-For is trivially forged by adding extra comma-separated values. The code takes only .split(',').next() which takes the leftmost value — an attacker behind a proxy can add a spoofed IP as the first value.

Also: bucket key 'unknown' (line 447) is shared across all clients without X-Real-IP. If many legitimate clients land on 'unknown' (e.g., behind a misconfigured proxy), they share a single 60 req/min bucket, creating a DoS vector where one client's traffic blocks all others classified as 'unknown'.
close_reason: All pentest fixes: require Signature header, verify Digest, enforce actor-keyId match, Date freshness, body size limit 256KB, webhook key via header, decompression bomb limits, symlink rejection, redirect following disabled, delivery SSRF recheck, backslash escape handling
